verbatim_text: It seems like the objective is very similar. Is the precise loss formulation different, or is there something else that's... I guess I don't have a good sense of thought. Okay. Let me expand all this detail. In classical RL, your output of learning is a policy. And concretely, you want this policy to give good reward over many draws of the solution. Good average reward. Over here, the output is not quite a policy. A policy is only a vehicle towards the ultimate goal of getting a good artifact, namely a good kernel, over here. And what kind of a policy would be a good vehicle towards this goal? Well, you want a policy that has actually very heavy tape. Even if the average reward is very, very bad, as long as over, let's say, 1,000 draws, the policy is able to produce one amazing solution, you're in good shape. Can I ask a follow-up?
